# macOS Updates

Production-ready one-command updater for Apple Silicon Macs. It keeps macOS,
App Store apps, Homebrew, native CLI/npm tooling, and 40+ internet-downloaded
apps current while updating only software already installed on the current Mac.

## Install

    /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/KasprowiczM/macOS_updates/main/install.sh)"

Manual setup:

    git clone https://github.com/KasprowiczM/macOS_updates.git ~/Dev_Env/macOS_updates
    cd ~/Dev_Env/macOS_updates
    bash setup.sh
    bash build_inventory.sh
    bash update_all.sh --dry-run -y

## What it does

`update_all.sh` runs seven layers in order:

1. Prescan installed apps into APPLICATIONS.md
2. macOS system update via softwareupdate -ia -R --verbose
3. App Store via sudo mas upgrade plus AppleScript fallback
4. Native CLI/npm tooling: Node, Bun, npm globals, selected dev CLIs
5. Homebrew upgrade, casks, cleanup, and doctor
6. Internet apps through registered handlers
7. Postupdate inventory version bumps and UPDATES.md history

## Safety model

- Apple Silicon only; macOS 13+.
- The installer builds local inventory from this Mac.
- Missing supported apps are reported, not installed.
- Private files such as APPLICATIONS.md, UPDATES.md, .env, preferences, and
  dev_sync config stay out of public Git.
- `softwareupdate` must keep `-R`.
- `mas upgrade` must run with `sudo`.
- Bash code stays compatible with Bash 3.2.

## Links

- Documentation: https://macosupdates.dev/docs
- Features: https://macosupdates.dev/features
- Source code: https://github.com/KasprowiczM/macOS_updates
